expectations.text: Design options (Option A, Option B, etc.) are presented with pros and cons
expectations.evidence: Step 3 presents 4 options (A: Direct Query Rewrite, B: ORM/Query Builder, C: Compatibility Layer, D: Schema-First with pgloader) each with Pros and Cons.

expectations.text: User is asked to choose an approach before plan is written
expectations.evidence: Step 3 ends with 'Which approach would you prefer? Or would you like to discuss trade-offs further?' Workflow correctly blocks at Step 4 waiting for user selection.

expectations.text: Questions are asked to resolve unknowns rather than assuming
expectations.evidence: Step 2 asks 6 questions covering which part uses SQLite, schema details, migration drivers, ORM existence, deployment environment, and downtime requirements.

expectations.text: Plan includes migration and rollback considerations (Be Practical philosophy)
expectations.evidence: Step 4 proposed phases include 'Phase 5: Data Migration' and 'Phase 7: Cutover & Rollback Plan'. Though full plan was not reached, rollback is explicitly part of the structure.
